channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: >if my 22 year old son is still living at home , I don’t him paying rent, I want him OUT — 42 replies — 168.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>if my 22 year old son is still living at home , I don’t him paying rent, I want him OUT

Open thread • Replies: 42 • Updated: 2026-07-02 12:22 UTC

OP

>if my 22 year old son is still living at home , I don’t him paying rent, I want him OUT

Early replies (first 3)

62433628
But saaaaaar isnt this normal in india and all civilized places? Only in the so called united states of america they kick their kids out

62433637
>>62433628
anything that isnt jewish is indian because.... because it just is, okay!!!!

62433641
All you need is a half million dollar down payment and you can get your mortgage payment down to $4,250 per month on your loan against a million dollar house. Then you can live in the desert on top of your neighbors with trees that will fall over during the first rains and slight breeze because they don't belong in a desert.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62435510
>>62434100
He paid for all his children's college tuition and housing. He's admitted in on his show. All the "advice" he gives is contrary to everything he's done himself to get ahead

62435715
>>62435510
how is not getting his kids into debt contrary to his advice of not getting into debt?

62435721
>>62433626
My parents supported me until I was 28. Now I’m a cpa making $150k and own a house and have 3 kids. Who knows where I’d be if they kicked me out at 22. Probably dead.

62435864
>>62435721
Well my family is rich but I was kicked out at 17 when I graduated early and I refused to pay rent(they wanted the going rate in my CA city)
My filling fell out 3 weeks ago and I need a root canal. But I just became a dad so all my money goes toward that. My first kid died a few years ago so, I am trying to be optimistic. I am 34

62435880
>>62433626
>I don’t him paying rent

62435903
>>62434079
(((starter homes))) are a made up buzz phrase to psyop people into reading lying jewish news articles about (((real estate))) and the (((housing market)))

62435925
>>62433626
Normal or rich people don’t do this. A 22 year old is like 1-2 year college.

62436306
>>62435715
his repeatedly told callers that their adult children shouldn't be living with them and that they should be paying rent. He didn't charge his kids rent

62436453
>>62433652
you seen it here first folks, newest rapper on the block dropping the heat

62436581
>>62435510
Boomers, and some older GenX (like Dave), really are out of touch when it comes to the importance of parental assistance.
The era of bootstrap-pulling is long over, and any mildly observant millennial or zoomer has probably noticed by now that their successful peers almost always got help (and a lot of it) from mom and dad.
And it's never been easier to notice this, given how over-exposed people are, thanks to social media, wikip etc.

62436586
>>62435864
Your family can’t be that rich. There’s just no way they’d kick you out at 17 if they were. It’s just not something actually rich people do.

62436615
>>62436306
his callers arent financially stable people whose lives are going well lmao

62436749
>>62435864
>filling fell out
>>62435864
>need a root canal
>>62435864
>first kid died
>>62435864
>optimistic
nigga brush your teeth and put down the meth pipe

62436842
>>62436586
Get a job you little brat.

62436889
>>62436581
My uncle offered to help me get a job and then never called me back. My mom completely refused to help me because "nepotism". Boomers ruined my life.
>get a lot of it
I knew a girl who was whining she couldn't afford rent because she divorced her black husband for beating her, then talked about how her parents gave her 'only' 100k for a downpayment on a house.

Lol.

62436891
>>62436306
He literally gave all his kids from different marriages manager jobs at his company.

>>62436615
So they should make their kids' lives shitty too?

62436914
>>62436581
>The era of bootstrap-pulling is long over
It isn't, no matter how hard your lazy entitled ass wishes it were.

62436916
>>62436889
>My uncle offered to help me get a job and then never called me back.
So why didn't you call him back? Show a little initiative for once in your life.

62437070
>>62436889
>”WAAAAHHHH MOMMY HELP ME HELP ME MOMMY!!!”
Lmao I always feel like such a chad when I come to this site.

62437244
>>62436914
>>62436916
>>62437070

62437592
>>62433626
My parents were shocked when I told them I was moving out at age 26. I had finished grad school and secured a 6 figure salary career but they still wanted me to live with them rent-free. White people are weird as fuck.

62437754
>>62434079
>starter home
nigger if its not a mansion then its just a fucking home
retarded buzz words trying to normalize the destruction of the middle class aren't real

62437769
>be zoomer
>starter homes costs 50 years salary

62437771
>>62437754
Starter homes are a thing just like starter cars

62438138
>>62436581
Ramsey is a boomer.

62438159
he gave all his kids fake email jobs in his company
what else is there to say

62438420
>>62435117
>100k saved when earned 189k last 3 years
could be better, could be worse. Actually it is decent because you saved over 50%. I saved up 55k at 22 already while working for a year now. Mind you, it is in a different currency because I'm an europoor.

My internship I had for half a year paid 47k/year so like 3900 a month. The second one now pays 5k/month and I try to save 3k for a house, put 1k into stock market and the rest are for expenses.

So yeah, also not bad but not perfect since apartments here cost like 500-800k easily. I just don't want to be like all my friends who plan to move out after 30.

62438432
>>62438420
What internship in europe is paying 3900 a month?

62438454
>>62433626
Not sure if kikes do this but it's just so lowly, stingy and jewish to charge rent to your kids. What are you, poor?
My parents had me while I saved to buy up front no jew mortgage. I'll never charge my kids either, I don't want their money and I don't prefer being alone, I love my family and all that. I'll also leave them generational wealth like my father will, but I'll teach them to be entirely self-sufficient and build wealth by the time they even see the number.

62438474
>>62436891
if he gave everyone else jobs except you, that would clearly say that it was your problem that prevented him, maybe you should ask. Aspergers

item.title: BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan? — 72 replies — 133.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan?

Open thread • Replies: 72 • Updated: 2026-07-02 11:55 UTC

OP

BTC is down again, but are you buying the dip or waiting for lower prices?

>Do you think this is just a normal correction, or is there more downside ahead? What's your strategy?

Early replies (first 3)

62426518
I've been shorting this garbage all month

62426527
>>62426518
Wow, you've got good profit by now then?

62426535
>>62426527
up 80% on some 2x short BTC etf

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62433751
>>62433616
you know nothing at all

62433844
>>62433598
Back at ya anon. Wishing you much WAGMI

62433854
>>62426512
I'm not retarded so I'm never buying BTC for any price. Being a cryptotard in 2026 is even more pathetic than being a /pmg/tard

62433884
>>62433854
Your lack of retardness coupled with no faith in yourself is why you will never WAGMI

62433961
>>62426512
I don't think about BTC. It was created by centralized exchanges to attack Bitcoin. You're only talking about it because they printed a bunch of tether to get the price to $100k. There are no users, only a small group of poor speculators paying actual USD for this coin. The rest of it is tether iou's.

62434984
>>62433746
Yeah, it's real. Go look into it if you're skeptical. They even have their TBVs in public testnet now.

62434990
>>62433844
Twins?! Who

62434999
>>62433844
Twins?! Who's your bet? Lol

62435029
>>62433961
If there were really no users, I don't think we'd still be seeing institutions, ETFs, and developers building around Bitcoin.

62435427
>>62433884
Why do you like sending beauty women

62437132
>>62432200
It's just another hype

62437198
>>62435029
Are ETFs manipulated?

62437208
>>62434984
What's tbv?

62437216
>>62427317
Money printing obviously

62437422
>>62426512
Continue to troll any and all retards who hold any kind of crypto.

62437563
>>62437208
Basically a way to use native BTC as collateral without wrapping it or handing custody to someone else.

62437565
>>62437198
Every large market gets influenced by big players. The hard part is separating normal market activity from actual manipulation.

62437571
>>62437422
Seems like a waste of energy, but you do you.

62437623
>>62426759
nice my fren

62437628
>>62437563
I don't think it's a good idea

62437631
Btc is going 1million

62438074
>>62437628
What part worries you the most? For me, it comes down to the risk model. I'd be way more skeptical if I had to hand my BTC over to a custodian.

62438081
>>62433854
Time will tell which of us got it right.

62438118
>>62426537
cool, sounds like mumus will have a "great" time.

62438272
>>62438118
What's that?

62438282
>>62426512
I soldeded months ago. I dont believe in btc and never had. BTC couldnt even outperform motherfucking spy for the past 5 years so dont give me """""zoom out""""". more money to be made in stocks anway, no reason to be married to bitcoin.

62438284
>>62426512
>What's your strategy?
Apeing every goycheck.

62438322
>>62437565
ngmi

62438328
>>62438284
Buy kendu and ansem

62438410
>>62438328
No.

item.title: Have you capitulated on Chainlink yet or are you holding to 0? — 24 replies — 96.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Have you capitulated on Chainlink yet or are you holding to 0?

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-07-02 12:23 UTC

OP

Have you capitulated on Chainlink yet or are you holding to 0? If it's not obvious by now, LINK isn't used or needed for anything, and doesn't give you any sort of stake in the Chainlink company. It's effectively a meme coin with an illusion of "fundamentals".

Early replies (first 3)

62433176
>>62433164
Yes I’m the last stink marine on /biz/ that’s held out and I finally capitulated. You can tell big serg he can let it rip now.

62433183
>>62433164
Don't care not selling

62433185
>>62433164
Just sold everything. Good job anon, this was the post that finally did it. Congratulations.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62433307
>>62433164
Arsh goyal saaar pls, you can shit out 4000 of your erc20 scamtoken daily, nobody is buying your 65% team funding bags!

62433436
>>62433164
we are the cuckold marines
and we're NEVER selling

62433437
>>62433436
TRVE!!

62433455
>>62433185
Kek

62433461
>>62433307
A linkie acting like someone calling it out is a thirdworld scammer is an unfathomable level of projection.

62433700
>>62433164
I actually told myself at the start of 2025 that I would sell at the end of 2026 if it hadn't mooned yet.
So you have 6 months, peelers.

62433709
>>62433164
i sold

62435604
Adem won. Total gooner victory

62436081
>>62435604
DO NOT UNDERESTIMATE THE POWER OF ABSOLUTE GOONING

62437170
holding to $0, probably next peak will be $12 before total obliteration

CLL will survive and thrive, but token baggies are now obsolete

62438011
>>62433164
I was one of the never sellers but when they dropped build and allowed no comment on it or staking or payment abstraction failure and we hit $7 most recently I sold. I thought there was a structure being built out in the shadows but it really just seems like they actually are jewing link holders. I had a nightmare it crashed to $2 and sold the next morning because I would never be able to forgive myself if that happened and it honestly seems like it will. I can't believe I sold after putting thousands of hours into this and I really don't know what to look forward to anymore. My average was $21 and selling where I did after that much time and consideration and still losing so bad and surrendering the certainty of making it in such a big way was not joking one of the most painful things I've ever experienced, but things are what they are and I am not going to change them by losing more of my fucking money. It's weird to say but I perversely almost wish I held just so I could look forward to it a little bit longer. I have no big deliverance or catalyst on the horizon to look forward to now and that is a thousand times worse than the money I lost, which is itself substantial.

62438014
>>62438011
Copied word for word with zero edit a friend sent to another friend they'd tried to shill it to who I made sure didn't buy it. I tried to get the writer to sell too but he would not hear it and actually stopped hanging out with me because of it.^ Lol

62438027
>>62438011
Rough. I sold 4k stinks and realized 20k loss, but I’m wanting to DCA again. Guess I’m a masochist. Got 1k suicide stack left rn

62438189
>>62438027
Sorry for your loss man. I would consider taking a different route this time around if prices across all asset classes crash the way they seem like they will. You're getting a new chance to not make the same mistake.

62438207
>>62433164
Forget it—Sir Gay's fans are morons who can't even be called investors; but constantly talking about it doesn't help the forum move on, either.

62438211
Yeah but im just not gonna sell

62438239
>>62438011
kwab
nice AI fanfiction tho

>>62438211
ditto

62438361
>>62438239
What even vaguely makes you think this is AI outside the fact that it goes against your cult groupthink? I don't expect it to change your thinking itself, but I promise it is not AI. Lmao You need to realize that the world is not narrativized by your own headcanon and you are not situated to editorialize the parts of it that don't fit your headcanon from existing just because they make you feel bad and make you want to instantly repress knowing them rather than form a worldview which comports with what is true and reconciles with them. How do you not see that you are literally in a cult?

62438406
>>62438361
>t. verbose AI abusing xrp shill who sticks out like a sore thumb because he's a total retard

62438481
>>62438406
Lol What? I never used AI whatsoever nor did I try to claim to not be me. Nothing I wrote is even verbose and I am not the person who wrote it, it is a literal text from a friend I tried to help get out who ate hard losses. You have gotten SO weird.

item.title: I don't even know what my job is anymore. — 23 replies — 92.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I don't even know what my job is anymore.

Open thread • Replies: 23 • Updated: 2026-07-02 12:22 UTC

OP

>Be me
>29
>worked at company for 4 years
>Started off in IT operations
>After 2 years reorg changed my role into a service management analyst
>Doesn't really make sense but it will soon
>"trust me bro" - manager (who's empire is collapsing)
>doesn't work
>2 years later new department director
>Knows it's all BS and disempowers us to even do it
>Now I have nothing to do at all
>Now my manager scrambles for new work for us to do
>Just comes up with new thing
>Basically he's trying to justify his own existence because he's down to 5 staff, which is the minimum needed at this company

I'm extremely demoralised. Has this happened to anyone else? I don't even know what job I can get elsewhere because I've spent so long at this retarded company doing fake work hoping it would start to make sense and it never does

Early replies (first 3)

62436031
>>62436009
I work in a factory, with no AC, lost all of my overtime and my shift has gone from 18 workers down to 7 (other 11 were laid-off or fired).

62436037
>>62436031
Rough.

62436040
>>62436009
get out right fucking now if you know what's good for you

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62436055
if you haven't achieved a significant promotion (at least 40-50% salary increase) within 1-2 years of starting an entry level role (judging by the title this is an entry level role or close to it), you have been relegated to entry level hell and your salary adjusted for inflation will forever match what it was when you started, no matter how many lateral "promotions" they give you, until you're fired

speaking from experience as someone who has been promoted 4 times since starting an entry level role in 2020 and now managing the seniors that originally interviewed me

62436088
>>62436009
ive seen this many times in corporate offices. this is why you change companies every 3 years max. not only for the salary increase, but the skill increase. using your instance for example. imagine day 1 you are dropped into a new company and they ask you to partition and encrypt a drive. on paper you have 4 years of experience. but could you do it?

62436120
>>62436037
Oh, but my point in that response was, I've been doing the same factory wagie job for 15 years. Anytime I look at other jobs, they all pay less. Some even require a degree and pay less.

I haven't gained any news skills (except economic, finance, psychology and philosophy education from listening to podcasts and reading for 8 - 12 hours a day, 5 days a week), which are useless at acquiring a job. I've been offered higher roles, but the pay is shit. They offered me.a 50 cent raise to become the shift manager. I laughed in their faces.

62436629
>>62436055
Doesn't apply to me

>>62436040
Why? If they want to make me redundant they'd likely have to pay me about £20K because of the way my corporation is, and unions and employment legislation

>>62436088
Nope, I basically have no skills. Wasted my time here. You're right.

62436643
>>62436629
>brit
You should still apply for better jobs. Eventually your job will close your department and find a reason to fire you legally. The longer you wait the worse it will be.
I recommend moving to the USA economic zone.

62436759
>>62436629
you and your management chain are both useless. your skills are atrophying and you'll be let go inevitably, whether by being fired or laid off. at that point you won't have a job while you try to find the next job which makes it infinitely harder and more stressful

62437195
>>62436031
I worked at a warehouse with no AC just huge cattle fans in the summer. They would prop the doors open and blow the hot air in. On the hottest day the fuckhead manager would make gatorade. The boss/managers/whore secretaries would sit in the offices all day on those hot ones never coming out to the floor.

62437377
>>62437195
Ours do the same thing.

In our meeting they were like. We know it's 95 degrees in the factory and the dew point is 73, so we're giving you guys an extra 5 minutes of break time to cool off!

No, I'll take breaks whenever I want, retard.

62437391
>>62437195
>>62437377
Oh, and someone was already taken away by an ambulance yesterday because they passed out from heat exhaustion.

62437394
>>62436629
Sounds like you're unironically in a position that is kept around solely for an incoming executive to look good by cutting staffing costs. Similar to those retarded women everyone makes fun of at tech companies that get laid off every 6-18 months. That's you right now. Enjoy getting paid for doing nothing for a while and buff up your portfolio/resume.

62437477
>>62436055
I got to 2 years promotion with good raise but I think I’m gonna change companies next move because these guys treat me like shit and have no respect for anyone. It’s still hard though I feel like getting a damn interview somewhere is just so hard to get.

62437891
>>62436009
figure out what you can do and blast resumes

62437953
I know that feel. I work in a non technical role in relation to software and I have had very little to do for the past few years.

I think 20-30 years ago there was some justification for our types of jobs because software development was less commoditised, more confined to gigaspergs, took longer, failed more, etc. But all that shit surrounding the software engineers themselves has zero justification now.

62437964
>>62436009
>oh no i get a paycheck for doing nothing
>my life is so hard
fuck off and kill yourself niggerfaggot

62438012
>>62436009
It doesn't matter. Just get paid and go home

62438053
>>62437195
I hate people who open windows and doors during heat waves

62438414
>>62436643
I agree, I need an out, but firing me legally at this company is basically impossible for them as long as I'm meeting expectations, which have to line up to whats in my original contract, it's the one good thing about the job. If I could move to the US easily I would.

>>62436759
You're absolutely right, thats where my anxiety is from

>>62437394
Yep, that's it basically. Slightly more nuanced but that's the crux of it without boring you with extra info.

>>62437891
The only thing I've really developed is soft skills, but maybe that's more valuable in a world full of asocial TikTok brain autists where even former normies have lost the ability to communicate with one another

>>62437964
I know what you mean it's a good problem to have but planning for the future is major anxiety because I feel like a bubble waiting to burst.

62438434
>>62436009
just look for another job while making money on this one, you've had 2 years.

62438446
I also don’t have any work at my job

So I play video games all day, easiest six figs of my life, maybe eventually they find out but I’ve been doing this five years now with good annual reviews and 5% bonuses so whatever

I actually got thanked during the winter for staying late hours and it was because I forgot the time during my Elden bring replay

62438477
>>62436088
>partition and encrypt a drive
Takes 5 minutes of research on Google to figure out how to do if you don't know already. Anybody that can't figure this out is retarded.

item.title: LET'S FUCKING GOOOO — 22 replies — 88.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
LET'S FUCKING GOOOO

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-07-02 12:22 UTC

OP

LET'S FUCKING GOOOO

Early replies (first 3)

62435458
gz

62435459
>>62435387
fake email

62435472
>>62435387
>>62435458
>>62435459
Sir please kindly, have you resolved the issue sir?

What you need is a blockchain expert that can help you retrieve the funds sir.

Have you heard about Mis Petunia Rose Tulips? She has helped me turn my 0.3 BTC into 2.8 BTC. She has been a blessing to me and my family.

Do you have t3l.eg.r4m sir? Please kindly do the needful and install Teamviewer to help her assist you sir.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62435480
>>62435472
Wow <i'm surprised someone else here also knows miss Petunia Rose Tulips. I started with only $5000 and she has turned it into $17000 overnight. It really works!

62435533
>>62435480
I cant believe how good it worked. I only needed to connect the bot to my wallet and within days she made me so much money.

62436108
>>62435387
My portfolio breached 200k today.

62436129
>>62436108
>Screeching fuddie noises

62436130
>>62435472
>t3l.eg.r4m
is that word filtered?
telegram

62436143
>>62435472
Woah 9x roi, your village must be proud of you saar. I had a similar experience but she told me to smear cow dung across my face or else the trades would not go through, was this your experience too?

62436163
>>62435472
Profitable trading requires experience, a solid strategy, and preparation, which we lack. Understanding buy/sell ratios, identifying market leaders, and diversifying are key to reducing risk.
I've Been following miss Petunia Rose Tulips for a while now, and what stand out is how clearly she explains investing. No hype, no pressure-just real guidance that helps people think long term and make a smarter decisions.

62436564
>>62436130
The exchange jews do not want buyers/sellers to P2P via the chat/PMs on their platforms since it would bypass their fees, so they filter word any contact infos and channels.

62437240
>>62435533
Sir I recommend Mr. Johnson on WhatsApp thanks to his trading algorithms my family making $11550 per week passively.

62437245
>>62437240
Who is this mr. Johnson on WhatsApp? I am very interest i think I will contact him and start my financial success..

62437253
>>62437245
Yes I can safely assure you dear. Mr. Johnson is highly successful trader he has made my dreams come true financially. He is legit

62437328
>>62437240
>>62437245
>>62437253
does this faggot indian not know about ids or is it just a bot?

62437351
>>62437328
newfag

62437483
>>62437351
poorfag

62437566
>>62437483
fag

62437572
>>62437253
Mr. Jhonson "White Man" Smith is always giving the bullish cryptocurrency advices

62438318
>>62436108
are you a literal retard?

62438467
>>62438318
>up another 10k
you tell me faggot

62438473
>>62436108
>portfolio screenshot thread
it's over

item.title: LE FUTURE OF FINANCE ANNOUNCED — 20 replies — 80.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
LE FUTURE OF FINANCE ANNOUNCED

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-07-02 12:19 UTC

OP

Centralization won

Businesses across industries have signed up to use Open USD, including:

Visa, Stripe, Mastercard, American Express, Discover, Fiserv, Adyen, Cloudflare, Corpay, Jack Henry, Klarna, Affirm, Ramp, OnePay, Brex, Checkout.com, WEX, PayPay Corporation, Western Union, Nuvei, Remitly, Ria, Marqeta, MoneyGram, Nium, Worldline, Galileo, Highnote, i2C, Lithic, Thredd, Episode Six, Verituity, Félix, Taptap Send, CAL (Israel Credit Cards)
BlackRock, BNY, Standard Chartered, Commonwealth Bank of Australia, Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group, Intercontinental Exchange, National Australia Bank, DBS, U.S. Bank, BBVA, Mizuho Financial Group, Shinhan Financial Group, Westpac, Itaú, OCBC, ANZ, UOB, Chime, Banco Bradesco, Huntington Bank, Citizens Bank, KB Kookmin Card, Emirates NBD, Hanwha Group, Banorte, Bank Hapoalim, FNB South Africa, K Bank, SoFi, Woori Card, Absa, Kakao Bank, Samsung Card, Bank Leumi, Wenia by Grupo Cibest, Nedbank, Isbank, Abu Dhabi Islamic Bank ADIB, Banco de Crédito del Perú, Mashreq, RAK Bank, Hana Card, Hyundai Card, BCcard, Cross River, Grupo Aval, Davivienda, The Bancorp, Pathward, Banca Transilvania, Nonghyup Card, Neo Financial, Maya Bank, Netbank, Freedom Bank Kazakhstan, Lead Bank, Kapital, MAX
Google, Samsung Electronics, IBM, Shopify, Mercado Libre, Mercado Pago, Infosys, DoorDash, Wix, Grab, Rakuten Group
Cuckbase, T*mp*, B*b*t, S*l*n*, OKX, R*ppl*, Cr*pt*.com, Fir*bl*cks, G*m*n*, M*t*M*sk, A*v*, *T*r*, G*l*x*, Dunamu, Ledger, M**nP*y, Anchorage Digital, Digital Asset, Tr*st W*ll*t, Meow, M*rph*, Eth*r.F*, Lightspark, zerohash, B*ts*, Bridge, Rain, BVNK, Mesh, Privy, B*tg*t Wallet, StraitsX, Yellow Card, Reap, Brale, RedotPay, Immersve, St*ll*r, P*l*g*n, *pt*s L*bs, Pl*sm*, K*ST, Bl*ss*m, L*m*n

Early replies (first 3)

62431357
>No chainlink


its over boys and bots and jeets

62431362
no link in sight btw

62431419
>>62431347
on which blockchain? they mention Solana but there's no way that they issue so much money on this chain while they're losing validators daily.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62431439
why woudl I want to use the stablecoin controlled by payment processors already accused of fucking people over though

62431514
>>62431347
>>62431439
right now your options are completely centralized entities like circle and tether

62431542
The OpenUSD name is already taken by a 3D file format.

62431566
>>62431357

62438465
Hmm I wonder what would OUSD and the businesses using it need for OUSD to be a serious institutional-grade stablecoin?

What premium infrastructure are those businesses listed already using?

To make OUSD work and be a serious competitor to Circle they'll need:

>Multichain Price+datafeeds with an oracle that is not going to experience a sudden blackout or hack
>Compliance engine that matches LEI standard
>Proof of reserve
>Orchestration and automation

... Hmm what could it be??

item.title: I'm broke as fuck, right now trying to learn some FOREX shit but it takes too much time to learn all the necessary shit… — 6 replies — 24.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I'm broke as fuck, right now trying to learn some FOREX shit but it takes too much time to learn all the necessary shit…

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-07-02 12:26 UTC

OP

I'm broke as fuck, right now trying to learn some FOREX shit but it takes too much time to learn all the necessary shit so what else can I try to stop being broke ass? What made y'all 6 figures or more?

Early replies (first 3)

62432407
Going to my job for a year and some change

62432477
Train up and join the military at the beginning of the next fiscal year. Join a trade like elevator mechanic. Do something with ai-arbitage, idk man.

62432500
>>62432477
Let me guess. Loser dad?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62432524
>>62432371
Being a better person made me a six figure income in fiscal year 2025. God helps you pay your bills if you are making a sustained effort at being a better person. Start by making a list of your worst qualities and behaviours.

62436100
>>62432371
What's the problemerino, forex-seeking anon?

62438490
>>62432371
son, you need to learn about the twin miracles:
Grit & Compounding

Grit: Slaving away as a wagie and when you get off work either going to a second job, or learning some skills and keeping at it until you excape the hand-to-mouth syndrome. Only ~25% of people are able to do this in capitalist societies; more like 4% in non-capitalist societies.

Compounding: Would you rather have a million dollars today or 1 penny today compounded at 100% daily for a year? take the latter because you would have more than $5 million at the end of the month.

Point being - work hard, save what little you can, and invest it. It will take time, but you will see it accumulate in time. Remember the "rule of 72" - basically a 10% gain compounded yearly doubles your money in 7.2 years.

So - say you save $2,000 a year. in five years you will have $14,652
In 10 years, that's $40,250
In 20: $140,000

And then the other two best pieces of financial advice I know are: Don't get divorced & buy a duplex

item.title: You still have time — 10 replies — 8.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
You still have time

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-07-02 11:15 UTC

OP

270,000 BTC accumulated by whales at $59k. largest single accumulation spike ever recorded on chain. bigger than the covid bottom (150k BTC). bigger than the FTX bottom (180k BTC). ETF outflows hit $4.5b in june but that capital rotated to semis, not cash. the entities selling BTC face quarterly redemptions and compliance committees armed with citi's $82k target. the entities buying have no reporting requirements and no redemption pressure. LTH SOPR at 0.615 last printed in july 2023 at $25k-$31k before the run to $73k. forced sellers are finite. voluntary buyers at record size are not.

Early replies (first 3)

62436915
hahaha

62436931
>>62436892
That’s a big pop

62436933
>>62436892
no
not buying your retarded scam
the jig is up

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62436954
sorry mumu it's going to zero

62436965
>>62436892
>voluntary buyers at record size
no new individual is getting into crypto in 2026.

62436974
>>62436892

62437123
>>62436892
Not falling for the psyop ranjeet, we've still got a ways down to go

62437137
>>62437123

62437206

62438327
